channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Exclusive: World's Newest Country Is Without Medicine. And It's Getting Worse

media:content.media:description: MSF has publicly accused South Sudanese authorities of blocking humanitarian access.

item.description: For the people of South Sudan, the destruction of an MSF-run hospital meant the disappearance of the only secondary healthcare facility serving nearly 250,000 people across the region.
item.content:encoded: For the people of South Sudan, the destruction of an MSF-run hospital meant the disappearance of the only secondary healthcare facility serving nearly 250,000 people across the region.

item.title: The China Factor In The New India-US Critical Minerals Deal

media:content.media:description: US Secretary of State Rubio signed the deal in India

item.description: The United States and other nations depend heavily on China for these minerals. But in recent years, especially under President Donald Trump's administration, Washington has pushed to diversify its sourcing to reduce reliance on Beijing.
item.content:encoded: The United States and other nations depend heavily on China for these minerals. But in recent years, especially under President Donald Trump's administration, Washington has pushed to diversify its sourcing to reduce reliance on Beijing.

item.title: NASA Rolls Out Moon Base Plan, Signs Deals For Landers, Rovers, Drones

media:content.media:description: Carlos Garcia-Galan (L), NASA Moon Base programme executive outlines plans for NASA's "Moon Base"

item.description: The space agency outlined the first phase of its moon base plans on Tuesday, awarding hundreds of millions of dollars in contracts to four US companies.
item.content:encoded: The space agency outlined the first phase of its moon base plans on Tuesday, awarding hundreds of millions of dollars in contracts to four US companies.

item.title: Trump Team Proposes Non-Disclosure Agreements For Staff To Stop Media Leaks

media:content.media:description: The proposed notice seeks comment on several questions

item.description: A proposed notice is expected to be officially published seeking comment on a draft NDA to be used by federal agencies for "both new and existing employees."
item.content:encoded: A proposed notice is expected to be officially published seeking comment on a draft NDA to be used by federal agencies for "both new and existing employees."

item.title: Rise In Suicides Among Immigration Detainees Since Trump Took Office: Report

media:content.media:description: Nine of the deaths were of Hispanic men who had arrived in the US from four countries

item.description: An AP probe found that at least 10 detainees, all men, have died by suicide since President Donald Trump took office in January 2025, a pace that exceeds growth in detainee population
item.content:encoded: An AP probe found that at least 10 detainees, all men, have died by suicide since President Donald Trump took office in January 2025, a pace that exceeds growth in detainee population

item.title: "Bleeding India By Thousand Cuts": UN Envoy Slams Pak For Terrorism

media:content.media:description: Indian diplomat's strong remarks came during the Security Council debate

item.description: The Indian diplomat said India has every right to defend itself from such cross-border terrorism.
item.content:encoded: The Indian diplomat said India has every right to defend itself from such cross-border terrorism.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | The Quad Is Slowly Going Quiet - But For A Reason
item.description: The Quad is gradually moving away from high-profile summitry toward a quieter, more embedded form of strategic coordination.
item.content:encoded: The Quad is gradually moving away from high-profile summitry toward a quieter, more embedded form of strategic coordination.

item.title: Big Tech's AI Cost Panic: Uber, Microsoft And The Burn Problem

media:content.media:description: Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet and Meta are expected to have spent $320 billion on AI infrastructure in 2025

item.description: Big tech companies such as Microsoft and Uber are now raising serious questions whether the huge AI spends are justified after all?
item.content:encoded: Big tech companies such as Microsoft and Uber are now raising serious questions whether the huge AI spends are justified after all?

item.title: Florida Man Arrested For Stealing Rs 11 Lakh Worth Of Pokemon Cards Using Chainsaw

media:content.media:description: Suspect used chainsaw to break store glass and steal Pokemon cards worth $12,000.

item.description: A Florida man has been arrested for stealing $12,000 (Rs 11.4 lakh) worth of Pokemon cards using a chainsaw.
item.content:encoded: A Florida man has been arrested for stealing $12,000 (Rs 11.4 lakh) worth of Pokemon cards using a chainsaw.

item.title: Byju's Founder Gets 6 Months Jail In Singapore, Ordered To Pay $70,500

media:content.media:description: Byju's founder Byju Raveendran has been instructed to surrender himself to officials

item.description: Byjus founder Byju Raveendran is sentenced to six months jail by Singapore court for contempt.
item.content:encoded: Byjus founder Byju Raveendran is sentenced to six months jail by Singapore court for contempt.

item.title: Marco Rubio's Office Deletes "Stupid People" Post After Racism Remarks Spark Buzz

media:content.media:description: Rubio said he specifically asked the reporter who he was referring to, but he did not respond.

item.description: Though the reporter did not clarify, many saw his question as a reference to President Trump's recent endorsement of racist remarks made by American podcaster Michael Savage, who referred to India, China and some other countries as "hell-holes".
item.content:encoded: Though the reporter did not clarify, many saw his question as a reference to President Trump's recent endorsement of racist remarks made by American podcaster Michael Savage, who referred to India, China and some other countries as "hell-holes".

item.title: Russia Claims Breakthrough Vaccine Against New Ebola Strain

media:content.media:description: Russia's announcement of a vaccine against a new Ebola strain comes at a critical time

item.description: Russia has announced the development of a vaccine targeting a new Ebola strain linked to the ongoing Bundibugyo outbreak in Congo and Uganda. Experts say the move could strengthen global outbreak preparedness, though clinical data is awaited
item.content:encoded: Russia has announced the development of a vaccine targeting a new Ebola strain linked to the ongoing Bundibugyo outbreak in Congo and Uganda. Experts say the move could strengthen global outbreak preparedness, though clinical data is awaited

item.title: 'Illegal': India's Response To J&K References In China-Pak Joint Statement

media:content.media:description: India's strong remarks came in response to a joint statement released by Islamabad and Beijing

item.description: Foreign Ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said that New Delhi "opposes and rejects" Islamabad's "illegal and forcible occupation of India's sovereign territory."
item.content:encoded: Foreign Ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said that New Delhi "opposes and rejects" Islamabad's "illegal and forcible occupation of India's sovereign territory."

item.title: Israel Targets Hamas Military Chief, Who Led Intel During October 7 Attack

media:content.media:description: It was not immediately clear if he had been killed in Tuesday's strike

item.description: Mohammed Odeh served as head of Hamas intelligence during the October 7 massacre and was appointed approximately one week ago as successor to Ezzedine Al-Haddad
item.content:encoded: Mohammed Odeh served as head of Hamas intelligence during the October 7 massacre and was appointed approximately one week ago as successor to Ezzedine Al-Haddad

item.title: US-Iran War LIVE: Iran Calls US Strikes 'Bad Faith', Warns Of Consequences As Talks Continue

media:content.media:description: US-Iran War News LIVE Updates: The US Central Command said the strikes were conducted in "self-defence."

item.description: Iran War: The US military on Monday carried out strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and boats laying mines.
item.content:encoded: Iran War: The US military on Monday carried out strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and boats laying mines.

item.title: Ahead Of 80th Birthday, Trump Declares Himself In "Perfect" Health
item.description: Trump's doctor is expected to release a summary of the medical exam in coming days, although the amount of detail released is entirely at the White House's discretion.
item.content:encoded: Trump's doctor is expected to release a summary of the medical exam in coming days, although the amount of detail released is entirely at the White House's discretion.

item.title: US Senator Calls Pakistan's Role In US-Iran Talks "Problematic"

media:content.media:description: Republican Senator asked Pakistan to respond to Trump's call to join the Abraham Accords.

item.description: Senator Lindsey Graham's remarks came as Pakistan's Defence Minister Khwaja Asif said he is not in favour of Islamabad joining the Abraham Accords
item.content:encoded: Senator Lindsey Graham's remarks came as Pakistan's Defence Minister Khwaja Asif said he is not in favour of Islamabad joining the Abraham Accords

item.title: Cuba Appeals For International Help As US Intensifies Pressure

media:content.media:description: Things got worse when Trump cut off the supply of oil from Havana's ally Venezuela.

item.description: Donald Trump has openly mused about taking over communist Cuba, saying that after the US attack in January that ousted Nicolas Maduro in Venezuela, Havana could be his next target for military action and big change.
item.content:encoded: Donald Trump has openly mused about taking over communist Cuba, saying that after the US attack in January that ousted Nicolas Maduro in Venezuela, Havana could be his next target for military action and big change.

item.title: North Korea Tests New Multi-Purpose Missile Launch System: Report

media:content.media:description: The tests were overseen by the North's leader, Kim Jong Un, the state-run KCNA said.

item.description: South Korea said on Tuesday the North fired several projectiles, including a ballistic missile, off its west coast, the latest in a flurry of tests this year. Seoul said the missiles flew about 80 kilometres (50 miles).
item.content:encoded: South Korea said on Tuesday the North fired several projectiles, including a ballistic missile, off its west coast, the latest in a flurry of tests this year. Seoul said the missiles flew about 80 kilometres (50 miles).

item.title: Canada To Impose 21-Day Isolation For Travellers From Ebola-Hit Region

media:content.media:description: The World Health Organization said that more than 220 deaths have been reported so far.

item.description: Canadian officials also said that starting Wednesday, they are pausing final decisions on immigration applications for people from affected countries for 90 days.
item.content:encoded: Canadian officials also said that starting Wednesday, they are pausing final decisions on immigration applications for people from affected countries for 90 days.
